differences.title: 1. UPDATE MECHANISM
differences.leftDesc: Changes propagate directly to DOM elements that depend on them.
differences.rightDesc: Updates trigger when a reactive source changes and affects derived values.

differences.title: 2. GRANULARITY
differences.leftDesc: Tracks updates to individual values, minimizing re-renders.
differences.rightDesc: Often triggers re-renders for entire components or larger sections.

differences.title: 3. MEMOIZATION
differences.leftDesc: Reactions are automatically memoized based on signal dependencies.
differences.rightDesc: Requires explicit use of memoization techniques like `useMemo`.

differences.title: 4. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Efficient for frequent, small updates with less computation.
differences.rightDesc: Can become inefficient with many small, frequent state changes.

differences.title: 5. DOM MANIPULATION
differences.leftDesc: Signals can directly target and update DOM nodes.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on comparing virtual DOM trees for changes.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Associated with modern frameworks like Solid.js.
differences.rightDesc: Common in React, Vue, and other established libraries.

differences.title: 7. API COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Focuses on creating and consuming signals.
differences.rightDesc: Involves managing effects, memoization, and state within components.
